description: When a particular item finishes, the view should auto scroll. When an argument finishes, it should auto scroll.

results.summary: Made the Discover feed actually scroll when a card finishes playing
results.detailed_description: You reported that the feed should auto-scroll when an item/argument finishes — and it already had auto-advance logic (when a question card or argument plays to its end, the code moves to the next page and, at the end of a question's arguments, hands off to the next question). The problem was the scroll itself. The feed moved pages only by writing SwiftUI's .scrollPosition(id:) binding. That binding faithfully *reports* which page you're on, but writing to it is not a reliable way to *move* a strict paging feed (.viewAligned with limitBehavior .always): the paging wants a real drag to settle against, so a hands-free finish — a card ending with no finger on the screen — updated the internal 'current page' without the feed ever visibly scrolling. I wrapped the feed in a ScrollViewReader and now perform every programmatic move through proxy.scrollTo(pageID, anchor: .top). So when an argument finishes it scrolls to the next argument, and when the last argument of a question finishes it scrolls on to the next question's card. The same fix also makes the peek-and-bounce lock and the question handoff scroll reliably. I could not run it on a device from here (builds/deploys happen after hand-off), so please confirm on the phone that a finished card now carries the feed forward on its own.
results.identified_issue: Root cause: DiscoverView drove all programmatic scrolls solely by mutating the @State bound to .scrollPosition(id: $currentPageID). With .scrollTargetBehavior(.viewAligned(limitBehavior: .always)), writing that binding is unreliable for moving the feed absent a settling drag, so the hands-free auto-advance (added in 34cdba1) updated currentPageID but the ScrollView didn't scroll. The completion signal (ClipPreviewPlayer.finishedItemID) and the advance/unlock guards were verified correct for the hands-free happy path — question card -> each argument -> next question — so I deliberately left them untouched. Fix: ScrollViewReader + proxy.scrollTo on every currentPageID change; the .scrollPosition binding is kept for READING the listener's position (handleLanding needs it as the user swipes). scrollTo to an already-settled page (a user swipe) is a no-op, so this doesn't fight manual scrolling. If it still doesn't scroll on device, the next thing to check is whether finishedItemID is firing at all (e.g. Kokoro-render vs TTS-fallback completion paths) rather than the scroll mechanism. Unverified on device — no build from inside the flow.
